reasoning: The document exhibits catastrophic structural and content failures, particularly the truncation of approximately 90% of table rows across multiple vendor price lists. Major sections (Sophos and Trellix) were either omitted or rendered as unparsed images, and the document hierarchy has collapsed, nesting distinct vendor sections under one another. These failures make the price list functionally useless.

concerns.analysis: Several tables in the output are missing the vast majority of their rows compared to the source. Specifically, the 'Carahsoft Trellix Price List' contains only line item 10, omitting lines 1-9 and 11-29. The DLT CrowdStrike, SHI CrowdStrike, and Presidio Cisco tables each only contain the first data row, omitting approximately 90% of the information from their respective source tables.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Carahsoft Trellix Price List (Lines 1-29, pages 13-15); DLT CrowdStrike Table (10 rows, page 16); SHI CrowdStrike Table (9 rows, page 18); Presidio Cisco Table (10 rows, page 19).
concerns.output_quote: Table element following heading-12 contains only row 10; Table element following heading-15 contains only the 'Cloud Subscription' row; Table element following heading-16 contains only the 'Cloud Subscription' row; Table element following heading-17 contains only the 'Cloud Subscription' row.
concerns.location: Sections 2 and 3; table indices following heading-12, heading-15, heading-16, and heading-17.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The Sophos section (pages 5-9) and the first half of the Trellix section (pages 10-11) contain no structured text or table elements at all. All pricing and descriptive data from these pages are entirely omitted from the output's text content, appearing only as unparsed image placeholders.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Sophos Price List (Pages 5-9); SHI Trellix Price List (Pages 10-11).
concerns.output_quote: The sections 'SOPHOS' and the first part of 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)' contain only heading and image elements with no extracted tabular or paragraph text.

concerns.analysis: The 'Professional Services' section under the Trellix heading, consisting of a heading, a paragraph, and an unordered list, is duplicated in the JSON output. In the source document (pages 9 and 10), this information appears only once as a single continuous section. The JSON output includes these three elements twice in succession at indices 4-6 and 7-9 of the Trellix section's elements array.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 9 and 10 of the source PDF contains one section for 'Professional Services' starting with 'Security Engineer - Responsible for performing configuration/deployment...'
concerns.output_quote: In the 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)' section, elements at index 4, 5, and 6 are identical to elements at index 7, 8, and 9 (H4 'Professional Services', Paragraph 'Security Engineer...', and the subsequent List).

concerns.analysis: The document's top-level structural organization is corrupted. The 'CROWDSTRIKE' and 'PRESIDIO (CISCO)' headings represent major sections for different vendors, equivalent to 'BROADCOM' and 'SOPHOS'. However, in the JSON output, these sections are incorrectly nested as elements within the 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)' section. They should be distinct section objects in the root 'sections' array.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 14 starts the 'CROWDSTRIKE' section and Page 17 starts the 'PRESIDIO (CISCO) Price List' section as major vendor headings.
concerns.output_quote: The 'elements' list for the 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)' section contains the H2 heading 'CROWDSTRIKE' at index 14 and the H2 heading 'PRESIDIO (CISCO) Price List' at index 17.
concerns.location: Section 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)', elements at indices 14 and 17.

concerns.analysis: Multiple tables throughout the document have been severely corrupted or truncated. The CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form (p. 14), the SHI CrowdStrike Price List Summary (p. 16), and the Presidio CISCO Cost Proposal Form (p. 17) each only contain the first row of data in the JSON table objects, omitting approximately 90% of the table rows present in the source. Additionally, the Sophos tables (pp. 3-7) and the majority of the Carahsoft Trellix table (pp. 11-13) were converted into images rather than
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 14 (Crowdstrike table), Page 16 (SHI table), and Page 17 (Presidio table) show full tables with 8-10 rows of data each.
concerns.output_quote: The corresponding JSON table objects ('DLT CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form', 'SHI CrowdStrike Price List Summary', and 'Presidio CISCO Cost Proposal Form') each contain only a single data row.
concerns.location: Section 'TRELLIX (Formerly MCAFEE)', table elements at indices 15, 16, and 18.

concerns.analysis: The visual layout of the converted document is significantly compromised by the omission of large blocks of content that are present in the source PDF. Specifically, multiple sections for Sophos and Trellix price lists are represented in the HTML only by their headings, leaving the corresponding content areas empty. This creates a visual flow that does not represent the source's structure, where these sections contained extensive pricing tables. For example, the 'SHI (Sophos) Price List (Continu
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Pages 6-9 (Sophos price lists), 10-11 (SHI Trellix lists), 13 (Carahsoft Trellix lines 1-9), and 15 (Carahsoft Trellix lines 30-43).
concerns.output_quote: Repeated empty headers such as 'SHI (Sophos) Price List (Continued)' and 'SHI Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List' with no table content rendered beneath them.
concerns.location: Sophos and Trellix sections of the rendered HTML.

concerns.analysis: The document contains major table rendering failures. First, the Sophos price list is rendered as a static image capture from the PDF rather than as structured HTML table elements. Second, and more critically, the Carahsoft Trellix table is severely truncated; while the source PDF lists items from Line 1 through Line 43 across pages 13-15, the HTML only renders a single row (Line 10) before the table ends and the next section begins. This is a significant corruption of the table's presentation.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Page 5 (Sophos tables), Pages 13-15 (Carahsoft Trellix table lines 1-43).
concerns.output_quote: The Sophos section displays a single image of a table; the Carahsoft Trellix table only contains Line No 10: 'MV3ECE-AA-AI MVISION Mobile Advanced 1:1BZ'.
concerns.location: Sophos section image and Carahsoft Trellix table.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There are minor text errors and duplicates. In the Trellix section, the 'Professional Services' block (heading, paragraph, and list) is repeated twice. Additionally, there is a typo in one heading: 'CowdStrike' instead of 'CrowdStrike'.
concerns.output_quote: Professional Services section repeated (id heading-10 and heading-11); 'SHI (CowdStrike) Price List' (id heading-16).

action_items: Full document reprocessing is required as the current HTML output lacks the core data (pricing) that defines the document.
Investigate why the Sophos and Trellix tables were treated as images rather than structured HTML.
Fix heading hierarchy logic to ensure vendor sections remain at the root level.
